gene_description: Patatin-Like Phospholipase Domain Containing 3 — a membrane-bound enzyme involved in hepatic fat metabolism, triacylglycerol hydrolysis, and lipid remodeling. Variants disrupt adipose triglyceride lipase function, impairing hepatic fat mobilization and increasing NAFLD and fibrosis risk.

source.title: Genetic variation in PNPLA3 confers susceptibility to nonalcoholic fatty liver disease
source.finding: The I148M variant (rs738409-G) is strongly associated with increased hepatic fat levels (P = 5.9 × 10⁻¹⁰) and hepatic inflammation. Hepatic fat content is >2-fold higher in PNPLA3 rs738409[G] homozygotes vs. noncarriers. G allele most common in Hispanics (highest NAFLD risk group). Variant impairs hepatic fat mobilization and adipose triglyceride lipase activity.

GG.phenotype: PNPLA3 I148M/I148M homozygous — highest NAFLD/NASH/fibrosis risk
GG.effect: Homozygous for I148M variant. ~3.3-fold increased NAFLD risk, ~5-fold increased NASH and hepatic fibrosis risk. Markedly impaired hepatic lipid mobilization. Accumulation of triglycerides in hepatocytes. Increased inflammation and progression to cirrhosis. Severely reduced adipose triglyceride lipase activity.
GG.recommendation: Aggressive NAFLD management: weight loss (7-10% reduces fibrosis risk). Avoid alcohol completely. Monitor ALT/AST, FIB-4 index, and consider FibroScan annually. Consider pioglitazone or vitamin E if NASH diagnosed (biopsy-proven). Evaluate for hepatocellular carcinoma screening if cirrhosis develops. Semaglutide may provide benefit via weight loss and metabolic improvement. Avoid high-fat/high-sugar diet.

GC.phenotype: PNPLA3 I148M/M148 heterozygous — intermediate NAFLD risk
GC.effect: Heterozygous for I148M variant. ~1.7-fold increased NAFLD risk. Intermediate impairment of hepatic lipid metabolism. Increased hepatic fat content but not to the extent of homozygotes. Modest increase in inflammation and fibrosis progression risk.
GC.recommendation: Moderate lifestyle intervention: weight loss if overweight (5% reduction beneficial). Limit alcohol. Monitor liver function annually. Consider FibroScan if additional risk factors (metabolic syndrome, elevated transaminases). Mediterranean or low-fat diet. Regular exercise (150 min/week moderate intensity).

CG.phenotype: PNPLA3 I148M/M148 heterozygous — intermediate NAFLD risk
CG.effect: Heterozygous for I148M variant. ~1.7-fold increased NAFLD risk. Intermediate impairment of hepatic lipid metabolism. Increased hepatic fat content but not to the extent of homozygotes. Modest increase in inflammation and fibrosis progression risk.
CG.recommendation: Moderate lifestyle intervention: weight loss if overweight (5% reduction beneficial). Limit alcohol. Monitor liver function annually. Consider FibroScan if additional risk factors (metabolic syndrome, elevated transaminases). Mediterranean or low-fat diet. Regular exercise (150 min/week moderate intensity).

CC.phenotype: PNPLA3 M148/M148 wild-type — standard NAFLD risk
CC.effect: Homozygous for wild-type (M148) allele. Standard hepatic lipid metabolism. Normal adipose triglyceride lipase function. NAFLD risk driven primarily by metabolic factors (obesity, insulin resistance, diet) rather than genetic predisposition at this locus.
CC.recommendation: Standard preventive guidelines: maintain healthy weight, balanced diet, moderate alcohol, regular exercise. Monitor for metabolic syndrome. Liver function tests if other risk factors present.
